latest.title: Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 16:01
RNS Number : 7604F
Atlas Metals Group PLC
26 May 2026
26 May 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, announces that it has received a conversion notice from YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP ("
Lender
" or "
Yorkville
") under the convertible loan agreement ("
Funding Facility
") previously announced by the Company on 5 March 2026. Atlas further announces that it will additionally issue further ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") pursuant to the Company's
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
"; the
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
.
Funding Facility conversion
The Company has received a conversion notice from the Lender pursuant to the Funding Facility to convert £86,780.82 of the outstanding principal and accrued interest under the second loan of up to £2.0 million, as announced on 5 March 2026, into new Ordinary Shares at a price of 7.80 pence per Ordinary Share. Accordingly, the Lender will be issued with 1,112,574 new Ordinary Shares ("
Conversion Shares
").
At-the-Market Facility issue
In addition to the issue of the Conversion Shares, the Company further announces that 2,255,820 new Ordinary Shares ("
ATM Shares
", together with the Conversion Shares the "
New Shares
") will be issued at an average price of 8.795 pence per ATM Share, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026 all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of the Funding Facility.
All Ordinary Shares previously issued pursuant to the At-the-Market Facility have been placed and Axis currently holds no Ordinary Shares.
Chris Chadwick, Chief Executive Officer of Atlas Metals, commented:
"We are grateful to the continued support from Yorkville and the confidence they have demonstrated in Atlas Metals and our strategy through their conversion. The Company remains focused on completing the transformative acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd and I look forward to providing further updates in due course as matters progress."
Admission and total voting rights
Application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 3,368,394 New Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission is expected to occur on or around 27 May 2026.
When issued, the New Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 42,549,821 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 42,549,821. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

All other details remain unchanged.
The full amended text is shown below.

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG.L is a small UK‑listed mining exploration company focused on uranium and other base‑metal projects. Its main activity is to acquire, develop and potentially sell mineral assets, especially in the Central Asian region where it holds a Kyrgyz uranium lease.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is a critical component for nuclear power, and demand for clean energy is rising. AMG’s assets sit in a high‑potential region that could become a key supply source if political and regulatory hurdles are cleared. The company also has a track record of turning modest projects into larger deals through strategic partnerships.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the early stages of asset development, with heavy cash burn and a recent capital‑raising round. It has yet to generate revenue and is looking for offtake agreements or a joint venture to turn its uranium lease into a commercial operation. The stock is trading near the bottom of its 52‑week range, indicating a deep market sell‑off.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy filing a series of regulatory updates that paint a picture of a firm trying to keep the lights on: it held an AGM, issued new ordinary shares, secured a £2.5 million funding facility, and announced a possible acquisition. These moves suggest a focus on cash flow and future growth.

A second theme is the continued pursuit of a commercial partnership for its Kyrgyz uranium asset. Several filings hint at an upcoming admission document that may disclose offtake agreements or a joint‑venture structure.

A third thread is the company’s financial housekeeping – new shares, a change of auditor, and updates on a proposed acquisition – all pointing to a company in survival mode but looking for a catalyst to move from cash burn to cash flow.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical optimism – people are watching but not buying, waiting for a breakthrough.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None. No bullish chatter has emerged because the stock is too thin and the company’s fundamentals are weak.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many traders believe the company is stuck in a cash‑burn cycle with no clear path to revenue. The fear is that the uranium lease might never attract a buyer or that regulatory delays could stall the project.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment is flat – a few neutral posts about the AGM and funding, a handful of bearish comments about lack of progress. There is no clear movement toward optimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search interest, which is a classic sign that the market is ignoring the stock – a good thing for contrarians.

near_term_catalysts: The release of the admission document that may reveal a offtake agreement or joint‑venture deal for the Kyrgyz uranium asset.
A formal announcement of the £2.5 million funding facility being used to pay down debt or fund a pilot mine.
Positive updates from the proposed acquisition – e.g., a signed MoU or a share‑exchange agreement.
A spike in trading volume at the current support level (around £0.02) that indicates institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in daily volume (2‑3× the average), a price touch of the 20‑day moving average, or a statement that the company has secured a buyer for the uranium lease.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a deep crash. The APEX compression score suggests the price is being squeezed, so a mean‑reversion bounce could be imminent once a catalyst clears. Patience is required until the market sees a concrete partnership or funding use.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen over 80% from its peak, giving a huge upside if it can recover to 50% of that peak – a 100% gain relative to current levels. The APEX score indicates tight price compression, and the lack of any current catalyst means any announcement will be a shock. If the company secures a offtake deal or uses its funding to start a pilot, the price could surge dramatically, delivering the historic 162.8% returns seen in RED‑color signals.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no revenue, a heavy cash burn, and no confirmed buyers for its uranium lease. If the funding falls through or the project is delayed further, the share price could slide deeper, potentially losing the remaining 10% of its valuation. A worst‑case scenario is a 90% decline to the bottom of the 52‑week range.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: ~10% of current value (the remaining tail). Potential gain: ~100% if the stock rebounds to half its historical high. The reward far outweighs the risk, giving a 10:1 upside‑to‑downside asymmetry.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price touches the 20‑day moving average (~£0.02) and volume spikes 2‑3× the average. Set a stop 5% below that level (~£0.019) and size the position to 10% of your discretionary trading capital. Hold until the stock shows sustained buying pressure or a clear catalyst announcement.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for the admission document release or the funding facility announcement. If either occurs and the price moves above the 20‑day MA, consider a quick entry with a tight stop.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for delisting, bankruptcy, or a regulatory ban on its uranium lease, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AMG.L has crashed deep, is ignored by the market, and could rebound dramatically if it secures a offtake deal or uses its new funding – a classic high‑asymmetry contrarian play.
